content: Diagnosis requires peripheral blood and bone marrow evaluation. CBC often shows cytopenias (anemia, thrombocytopenia) with or without elevated WBC; circulating blasts suggest acute leukemia. Bone marrow aspiration/biopsy: ≥20% blasts defines acute leukemia (except APL with PML-RARA). Flow cytometry: immunophenotyping determines lineage (myeloid: CD13, CD33, MPO; B-cell: CD19, CD20, CD10; T-cell: CD3, CD7). Cytogenetics: karyotype identifies chromosomal abnormalities (prognostic in AML: favorable t(8;21), inv(16), t(15;17); adverse complex karyotype, -5, -7, TP53). FISH detects specific translocations (BCR-ABL for CML/Ph+ ALL, PML-RARA for APL). Molecular testing: NGS panel for mutations (FLT3, NPM1, IDH1/2, TP53 in AML; NOTCH1, SF3B1, del(17p)/TP53 in CLL). Minimal residual disease (MRD) monitoring by flow cytometry or PCR predicts relapse risk. Lumbar puncture for CNS involvement in ALL.
